page: Microsoft Azure D11 is a D-family virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores on an x86_64 architecture. The underlying processor is an Intel Xeon 8171M running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, featuring 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 14.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 102 GB, and the instance operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate that the hardware delivers entry-level performance across compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1062.29 and multi-core score of 2029.69 place the instance in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% of tested servers, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 177.78 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and approximately 23.27 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, reflecting a weak memory subsystem throughput. Similarly, the Geekbench single-core score of 853.0 and multi-core score of 1529.0 place this instance in the bottom 10% tier, indicating limited capacity for compute-intensive tasks such as software compilation or ray tracing.

Given the modest performance profile, the D11 instance is suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads that require a moderate memory footprint relative to CPU capacity. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, small database operations, and simple text processing tasks. The inclusion of 102 GB of local storage provides a baseline for local file caching or temporary data storage. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a low-cost entry point for legacy x86_64 applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation but do not require high-throughput memory or intensive multi-threaded processing.
description: The Microsoft Azure D11 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the D-family, featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 14.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB per core, alongside 102 GB of bundled local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance does not include GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates entry-level performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores placing the server in the bottom 10% to 25% tiers. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with uncached read speeds of 23.27 GB/sec. Consequently, the D11 is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, small database operations, and simple text processing, offering a low-cost, dedicated-core option for applications with modest resource demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D11 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 14 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, delivering entry-level performance for basic web serving and database tasks.

tagline: An entry-level, dedicated-core virtual machine with moderate memory and local storage for lightweight web serving and basic database tasks.
